description: Niacinamide, or as us normal people call itvitamin B3,is a real skincare superstar that has had so many studies and has so many skin benefits that we have to warn you that this "geeky details" description will not be short.

If you like to read about skincare (and if you are here, it seems that you do :)), we are sure that you have already read a lot about retinol. It is the ingredient everyone keeps talking about and there is a reason for that: it is themostproven anti-aging superstaravailable over the counter.
A form of skincare superstar,vitamin C. If you do not know why vitamin C is such a big deal in skincare, we have a reallydetailed, geeky descriptionthat's good to read. :)
If Vit C was a person it would be someone like Beyoncé, Jennifer Lopez or Mariah Carey. Serious performance,A-list superstarbut at the same time shows some major divaish behaviour.
Hyaluronic acid (HA) and its various forms are thecurrent IT-moisturizers. So let’s see, what is hyaluronic acid and even more importantly what’s the big deal about it?

It’s the - sodium form - cousin of the famousNMF,hyaluronic acid(HA). If you have ever struggled with let’s just call it “problem skin” (you know, acne, blackheads, whiteheads, large pores) then most probably you are familiar with salicylic acid (SA). Even if the name does not ring a bell, you have probably used it. Next to benzoyl peroxide, it’s thegold standard ingr

Unless you have avoided every bit of skin care info out there so far (in this case special welcome to you! :)) you must have heard of glycolic acid. Or at least aboutAHAs or to put it even more nice-soundingfruit acids.

Lactic acid is thesecondmost well-known andmost well researched among the AHAs. The no. 1 is glycolic acid. Ceramides make up about 50% of the goopy stuff that's between our skin cells and play a superimportant role in having a healthy skin barrier and keeping the skin hydrated. A reallyfamous peptide that is part of Matrixyl 3000, the most sold peptide complex in the word. Before we go and find out what the big deal with Matrixyl 3000 is, let's just focus on Palmitoyl Tripeptide-1 itself for a bit.

When it comes to sunscreen agents,Zinc Oxide is pretty much in a league of its own. It's aphysical(or inorganic)sunscreenthat has a lot in common with fellow inorganic sunscreenTitanium Dioxide(TiO2) but a couple of things make it superior even to TiO2.

At first glance, you could think that Bakuchiol is your averageplant extract. It is derived from the seeds ofPsoralea Corylifolia, aka Babchi, a plant important inIndian and Chinese medicine. It seems to us that squalane is in fashion and there is a reason for it. Chemically speaking, it is a saturated  (no double bonds) hydrocarbon (a molecule consisting only of carbon and hydrogen), meaning that it's anice and stable oily liquidwith a long shelf life.

Centella Asiatica - or sometimes also called Gotu Kola or Cica - has been used in folk medicine for hundreds of years. It’s traditionally used toimprove small wounds, burns and scratches and it’s also a well knownanti-inflammatory agentfor eczema.
Azelaic acid is a superstar acid with some serious magic properties. Before we list them out here's just a short intro.
Ferulic Acid (FA) is a goodie that can be found naturally in plant cell walls. There is a lot of it especially in the bran of grasses such as rice, wheat and oats.

If you are looking for a reason why red wine is good for you, good news, you have found it! Resveratrol, aka the "red grape antioxidant" is the thing that's suspected to keep the French from coronary heart disease despite their not so healthy eating habits (such as high saturated fat intake).
